title: Fixture: Gold-tier medium-criticality UC for tests
exclusions: Does not cover disk I/O saturation or memory pressure — fixture scope only.
evidence: Saved search fixture_gold_medium_mem.json with weekly PDF export to operations index.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Fixture demonstrates partial logging coverage mapping for gold-tier completeness tests.
compliance.controlObjective: Shows that memory pressure events are logged and reviewed on a defined cadence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Dashboard fixture_gold_medium_mem.xml plus saved search export archived weekly.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Host reports mem_used_pct above 85 for 30 minutes; search returns the host row.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Brief page-cache spike during backup stays under 30-minute average threshold; search returns zero rows.

dataSources: index=os sourcetype=vmstat from Splunk_TA_nix vmstat.sh (60s). Fields: mem_used_pct, host, _time.
app: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833), 9.x.

splunkbaseApps.name: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux

spl:
index=os sourcetype=vmstat host=*
| eval mem_used_pct=100-mem_free_pct
| timechart span=30m avg(mem_used_pct) AS avg_mem BY host
| where avg_mem > 85
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t avg(Performance.mem_used) AS avg_mem FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance.Memory BY Performance.host span=30m
| where avg_mem > 85
| rename Performance.host AS host
description: Tracks Linux hosts whose memory utilization averages above 85 percent for thirty minutes, highlighting pressure that precedes swapping and application restarts.
value: Memory pressure degrades batch jobs and web tiers long before OOM kills appear in logs. Early visibility lets operators recycle leaky services or right-size instances during business hours.
grandmaExplanation: We keep an eye on how full each server's memory gets over half-hour windows. When one stays too full for too long, we tell the team so they can free space before programs start crashing.
implementation: Enable vmstat scripted input on Splunk_TA_nix forwarders, route to index=os, schedule search every 10 minutes over 30 minutes, alert when any host exceeds threshold.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk_TA_nix on Linux UFs; mem_free_pct field extracted via TA props.conf.
• Role with index=os search permission.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable `[script://./bin/vmstat.sh]` interval=60, index=os, sourcetype=vmstat. Validate one event per host per minute.

Step 2 — Create the search
Use 30m span averages to ignore brief cache spikes. Understanding this SPL: mem_used_pct derives from TA-normalised free percentage.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare avg_mem to `free -m` on a sample host during steady state. Spot-check `_time` lag under 120 seconds.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add panel to Linux Health dashboard; default Last 24 hours; link runbook for service restart approval.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
Missing mem_free_pct: upgrade TA or verify props.conf merge. Flat 100%: check vmstat.sh permissions on hardened hosts.
visualization: Single-value hosts over 85% memory; timechart avg_mem by host; table with host, avg_mem, owning team from lookup.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux

knownFalsePositives: Backup agents and database cache warming can elevate memory without user impact; filter known batch hosts via lookup.
hardware: VM and bare-metal Linux in fixture lab.
